title: Iran Revolutionary Guards warn of 'long and painful strikes' on US warships, bases
contents:
# Iran Revolutionary Guards warn of 'long and painful strikes' on US warships, bases

## Overview
A senior Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) official issued a warning circulated through Iranian state media during the evening of April 30, 2026, declaring that any renewed US attacks against Iran will trigger 'long and painful strikes' targeting American warships and military bases across the region. The Independent and Jerusalem Post live blogs amplified the warning shortly before 22:00 UTC.

## Statement Highlights
- IRGC official cited as warning of sustained reciprocal strikes
- Targets explicitly include US warships in the Gulf and US regional bases
- Statement coincides with Iranian air-defence engagements over Tehran during the same evening
- Comments arrive ahead of Trump's Friday deadline either to end the war or extend it

## Threat Posture
The explicit reference to 'warships' aligns with prior Iranian warnings tied to the Strait of Hormuz blockade and follows Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's earlier same-evening declaration of Iranian 'new management' over the strait. Independent analysts cited by Dataminr-style early-warning aggregators flag the combined messaging as a credible indicator that Iran retains intent to escalate against US naval assets if attacks resume.

## Operational Implications
US Navy and Coalition Maritime Forces operating in the region likely tighten force-protection postures, particularly for capital units near the Strait of Hormuz. Insurance markets and AIS-tracked commercial transit volumes warrant monitoring for any further drop in Strait of Hormuz throughput.

## Tags
iran, irgc, warning, us-warships, persian-gulf, strait-of-hormuz, dataminr
summary: An IRGC official on April 30, 2026 publicly warned of 'long and painful strikes' against US warships and bases if Washington resumes attacks, amplifying Iran's earlier same-evening declaration of 'new management' over the Strait of Hormuz.
